recipes.title: Mohinga
recipes.description: Burmese national fish noodle soup with rice vermicelli, lemongrass-fish broth and crispy fritters.
steps: Poach catfish in water with smashed lemongrass for 15 minutes; remove fish, flake and reserve broth.
Pound onion, garlic and ginger into a paste.
Saute paste in oil with turmeric and paprika until fragrant.
Add flaked fish and cook briefly, then pour in fish broth.
Whisk chickpea flour with cool water and stream into the soup, stirring to thicken.
Add sliced banana stem, toasted rice powder and fish sauce; simmer 20 minutes.
Hard-boil eggs and halve.
Cook vermicelli, divide between bowls and ladle hot mohinga over with eggs, cilantro and lime.

recipes.title: Lahpet Thoke
recipes.description: Burmese fermented tea leaf salad with cabbage, tomatoes, fried beans and crunchy seeds.
steps: Rinse fermented tea leaves briefly, then mix with sesame oil and a pinch of salt.
Shred cabbage finely and dice tomato.
Toast sesame seeds in a dry pan until golden.
On a wide platter, arrange tea leaves in the center.
Around the leaves, place separate piles of cabbage, tomato, fried split peas, peanuts, garlic, sesame, shrimp and chillies.
Squeeze lime juice and drizzle fish sauce and a little oil over.
Toss everything together at the table just before eating.

recipes.title: Ohn No Khao Swè
recipes.description: Burmese coconut chicken noodle soup with egg noodles, crisp toppings and lime.
steps: Pound onion, garlic and ginger into a paste.
Saute the paste in oil with turmeric and paprika until fragrant.
Add diced chicken and brown.
Pour in chicken stock and simmer 20 minutes.
Whisk chickpea flour with cool water and stream into the soup, then add coconut milk.
Simmer 10 minutes until soup thickens; season with fish sauce.
Hard-boil eggs and halve.
Cook egg noodles, divide between bowls, ladle soup over and top with eggs, cilantro, scallions, crispy noodles and lime.

recipes.title: Burmese Curry
recipes.description: Slow-cooked pork curry with onions, paprika and turmeric, simmered in oil until rich and dark.
steps: Cube pork belly into 4 cm pieces.
Pound onion, garlic and ginger into a paste.
Heat oil in a heavy pot and fry the paste with paprika, turmeric and cayenne until oil turns deep red.
Add pork and toss to coat in spices, cooking 5 minutes.
Pour in water and add lemongrass, fish sauce, soy and tamarind.
Simmer covered for 1 hour.
Uncover and reduce until oil separates and floats on top, indicating the curry is done.
Skim a little of the spiced oil to spoon over rice when serving.

recipes.title: Shan Noodles
recipes.description: Burmese Shan-style rice noodles in a tomato and chicken sauce with garlic oil and pickled greens.
steps: Fry minced garlic in oil until deeply golden, then strain to make garlic oil and crispy garlic.
Saute chopped onion and ginger in some garlic oil with turmeric and paprika.
Add ground chicken and brown.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until they break down into a thick sauce.
Season with soy sauce and fish sauce; simmer 10 minutes.
Cook rice noodles, drain and toss each portion with a little garlic oil.
Top noodles with tomato chicken sauce, pickled mustard greens, peanuts, crispy garlic, scallions and cilantro.
Squeeze lime over before eating.

recipes.title: Khao Suey
recipes.description: Burmese curried chicken noodle bowl topped with crispy garlic, fried noodles, lime and chillies.
steps: Saute chopped onion, garlic and ginger in oil with turmeric and curry powder.
Add chicken and brown.
Pour in coconut milk and simmer 20 minutes.
Whisk chickpea flour with water and stream in to thicken; season with fish sauce.
Cook egg noodles separately and divide between bowls.
Ladle the chicken curry over the noodles.
Top with crispy fried noodles, fried garlic, peanuts, cilantro, lime and chillies.

recipes.title: Sigyet Khauk Swe
recipes.description: Burmese chilli oil noodles tossed with garlic-infused oil, fried shallots and pickled vegetables.
steps: Slice shallots and garlic thinly.
Fry shallots in oil over medium heat until golden, then drain; repeat with garlic.
Reserve the infused oil and stir in paprika and cayenne off the heat to make chilli oil.
Cook wheat noodles, drain well and divide between bowls.
Drizzle each bowl with chilli oil, soy and fish sauce.
Top with bean sprouts, pickled mustard greens, peanuts, fried shallots, fried garlic, scallions and cilantro.
Toss thoroughly and finish with lime.

recipes.title: Burmese Biryani
recipes.description: Burmese-style spiced chicken biryani layered with basmati rice, ghee, raisins and cashews.
steps: Marinate chicken in yogurt, paprika, turmeric, garam masala, garlic, ginger and salt for 1 hour.
Fry sliced onions in ghee until deep golden; reserve half for layering.
Add cardamom, cinnamon and bay leaves to the remaining onions.
Add marinated chicken and cook until browned and the marinade thickens.
Parboil basmati rice in salted water with a few whole spices for 6 minutes; drain.
Layer chicken and rice in a heavy pot, drizzling ghee, raisins, cashews and reserved fried onions between layers.
Cover tightly and steam over very low heat for 25 minutes.
Fluff with a fork and serve garnished with cilantro.

recipes.title: Falooda
recipes.description: Burmese-Indian iced dessert drink with rose syrup, basil seeds, vermicelli, jelly and ice cream.
steps: Soak basil seeds in water for 15 minutes until they bloom.
Boil agar agar with water and sugar until dissolved, then chill until set and dice into cubes.
Boil falooda vermicelli for 2 minutes, then rinse with cold water.
Chill milk thoroughly.
In tall glasses, layer 2 tbsp rose syrup, basil seeds, falooda vermicelli and agar cubes.
Add shaved ice and pour cold milk to fill.
Top each glass with a scoop of vanilla ice cream and crushed pistachios.
